title: carex hudsonii f. humilis
intro: Carex hudsonii f. humilis, also known as Hudson's sedge, is a type of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae). It is a low-growing, clump-forming perennial that is native to the northeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and treating respiratory issues. Carex hudsonii f. humilis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and providing antioxidant protection.
